_schema.description: Open research questions. Each has competing hypotheses, evidence for/against, status, and next steps.

question.en: Where is 'Mosina' — the town where David and Leah married, where Shimon was born, where they lived until 1939?
question.he: היכן 'מוסינה' — העיר בה התחתנו דוד ולאה, בה נולד שמעון, בה גרו עד 1939?
question.pl: Gdzie jest 'Mosina' — miasto, w którym pobrali się Dawid i Lea, gdzie urodził się Szymon, gdzie mieszkali do 1939?
question.fr: Où se trouve « Mosina » — la ville où Dawid et Léa se sont mariés, où Szymon est né, où ils ont vécu jusqu'en 1939 ?

candidates.label: Mosina near Poznań (Greater Poland)
evidence_for: Only 'Mosina' in 1933 Polish placenames index
Listed in Rapaport Research Institute descendants tree
evidence_against: No Jewish residents by 1930s (last 6 Jews left 1922)
1911 birth records do not include David
2 sawmills owned by Polish non-Jewish Władysław Litke and Władysław Urbaniak
Far from where Leah's family lived

candidates.label: Morszyn (spa town near Stryj)
evidence_for: Ms. Kasia (ŻIH) hypothesis
Near Bolechów and Dolina region
Spa town fits the 'rental hotel for Polish visitors' family memory
evidence_against: Phonetically a stretch from Mosina

candidates.label: Muszyna (spa town near Krynica)
evidence_for: Magda's recollection of past conversation with Dov Rapaport about this area
Spa town fits 'rental hotel for Polish visitors' memory
Phonetically closer to 'Mosina'
Near Krynica where Rebeka was confirmed in 1912

PRIMARY-SOURCE CONFIRMATION (2026-05-20): Lusia's memoir (סיפורה של לוסיה) explicitly names מושינה (Muszyna) as 'the small resort town' where she and David married in February 1938 (page 17) and where Lusia managed a hotel that hosted 'all the social cream' of the town's visitors (page 18). The memoir uses the Hebrew spelling מושינה consistently. See docs/research/memoir_extraction_2026-05-20.md.
evidence_against: Far from Lwów / Bolechów (but no longer disqualifying — the memoir explicitly places them there)

candidates.label: Mszana Dolna (Kraków Voivodeship)
evidence_for: Dana found family reference to 'Rapaports of Mszana' as 'forest people' (ludzie lasu) in timber trade
Memorial/Yizkor books reportedly mention 'Rapaport Sawmill' / 'The Masina'
Mountain area fitting the family wood-sawmill story
evidence_against: No Rapaport in 1930 industry directory there (but pre-1930 not yet checked)

2026-05-17 CC web research: Sztetl Mszana Dolna site enumerates every Jewish timber-trade family pre-WWII (Goldberger, Geller, Wiener, Feuerstein, Kleinmann, Frey, Wachtel, Weinberg, Faber) — no Rapaport. Dedicated descendant pages exist for Rebhun and Rotkopf families but no Rapaport page. Source: docs/research/h1_mosina_2026-05-17.md

candidates.label: Dolina (district seat near Bolechów)
evidence_for: Basia's hypothesis — phonetic/orthographic similarity (same length, same -ina ending)
Multiple sawmills 1930
Heart of Weinreb territory
evidence_against: Doesn't sound like 'Mosina' when spoken
Sawmill owners listed in 1930 are not Rapaports

next_steps: Check pre-1930 industrial directories for Mszana Dolna and Mszana area for Rapaport timber business
Check Yizkor books for Mszana, Morszyn, Muszyna referencing Rapaport sawmills
Cross-reference with Shimon's 1937 birth records — where exactly was he registered?
Ask Dalia (Dana, Doron and Daniel's mother) and the family for any specific landmark/feature memories (the river? mountains? lake? neighbors? language spoken on the street?)

question.en: What is Leah's actual date of birth — 8 April 1913, 6 April 1916, 8 April 1916, or ~1917?
question.he: מהו תאריך לידתה האמיתי של לאה — 8 באפריל 1913, 6 באפריל 1916, 8 באפריל 1916, או ~1917?
question.pl: Jaka jest rzeczywista data urodzenia Lei — 8 kwietnia 1913, 6 kwietnia 1916, 8 kwietnia 1916, czy ~1917?
question.fr: Quelle est la véritable date de naissance de Léa — 8 avril 1913, 6 avril 1916, 8 avril 1916, ou ~1917 ?

candidates.label: 8 April 1913
evidence_for: Bolechów civil birth certificate (1913)
evidence_against: Conflicts with family death certificate

candidates.label: 6 April 1916
evidence_for: Family death certificate
evidence_against: No civil document found for this exact date

candidates.label: 8 April 1916
evidence_for: ŻIH index lists 'Lusia Rappoport' b. 1916-04-08, parents Eljan/Mahylda — close to Eliyahu/Mathilde
evidence_against: May be different person; spelling unusual

evidence_for: Leah said she was 18 at marriage in 1935 (per memoir) → would imply 1917
evidence_against: Brides commonly understate age; the 1935 vs 1938 marriage date is also unclear

next_steps: Wait for the Lusia ŻIH registration card (Basia is requesting from Warsaw archives)
Look for sibling birth records to triangulate (Pnina's birth record not yet found)

question.en: Verify David's signature on the 1946 Brussels DP card against any later document
question.he: אימות חתימת דוד על כרטיס העקור מבריסל 1946 מול כל מסמך מאוחר יותר
question.pl: Weryfikacja podpisu Dawida z karty DP z Brukseli 1946 z jakimkolwiek późniejszym dokumentem
question.fr: Vérifier la signature de Dawid sur la fiche DP de Bruxelles de 1946 par rapport à tout document ultérieur

next_steps: Family search: Israeli customs authority records of David (he worked there post-war)
Family search: any letters, official documents, will, or photographs with signature
The DP card is the highest-trust evidence; signature match would settle remaining doubts

question.en: How did David survive 1939-1946? Did he serve in the Polish Army? Was he deported to Siberia? Did he escape via Anders' Army to Iran in 1942?
question.he: כיצד שרד דוד בין 1939-1946? האם שירת בצבא הפולני? האם הוגלה לסיביר? האם נמלט עם צבא אנדרס לאיראן ב-1942?
question.pl: Jak Dawid przeżył lata 1939-1946? Czy służył w Wojsku Polskim? Czy został deportowany na Syberię? Czy uciekł z Armią Andersa do Iranu w 1942?
question.fr: Comment Dawid a-t-il survécu de 1939 à 1946 ? A-t-il servi dans l'Armée polonaise ? A-t-il été déporté en Sibérie ? S'est-il évadé via l'Armée Anders en Iran en 1942 ?

hypotheses.context: The DP card has '1942' as the citizenship-strip / deportation date. Basia notes 1942 corresponds to Anders' Army evacuation through Iran (10% of evacuees were Jewish). Family memory: David escaped Nadwórna ghetto in a railway wagon, reached Lwów, worked railway construction. These don't fully reconcile — family memory has him in Lwów, DP card lists Lwów as 1938 residence and 1942 as the loss-of-status year.

candidates.label: Path A — Soviet deportation 1940 → Anders Army → Italy → West
evidence_for: Galicia was Soviet-occupied Sept 1939 - June 1941, so David was under Soviet rule then
Soviet April 1940 deportation wave specifically targeted FORESTERS as a category (Wikipedia: Soviet repressions of Polish citizens 1939-1946)
DP card lists David's Italian fluency — Anders Army Italian campaign (1944-45) explains where a Galician forester learned fluent Italian
Anders Army demobilization in Western Europe explains Brussels arrival 1946
Anders evacuation Aug 1942 (110,000 Poles, ~5,000-6,000 Jewish) matches the DP card '1942' citizenship-loss timing
evidence_against: Family oral history places David in Nadwórna ghetto escape in 1942, not Iran
No Polish military record yet checked
candidates.next_step: Apply UK MoD Polish Records office (NOR-PolishDiscOffice@mod.uk, £30, 1-month) — single most testable question

candidates.label: Path B — Stayed in German Galicia → Nadwórna ghetto escape → Lwów hiding → liberation
evidence_for: Family oral history is specific (railway wagon, hidden in niche between wooden logs, reached Lwów)
Nadwórna ghetto liquidation 24 Oct 1942 aligns with DP card '1942' as the year of deprivation
Forester profession would have provided access to timber transport for the escape route
Some Jews DID escape Nadwórna ghetto to forests / Hungary (Yad Vashem confirms)
evidence_against: Italian fluency is hard to explain on this path — where did he learn it?
Brussels arrival from Lwów 1944-46 is geographically less direct than Italy → West
candidates.next_step: Lwów Aryan-side construction worker registries 1942-44 in DALO; Leah's memoir for cross-references

candidates.label: Path C — Combination (e.g. deported & returned, or escaped & joined partisans)
evidence_for: Logically possible
evidence_against: Evidence-free at present
candidates.next_step: Trace by document trail across both paths

research_2026_05_17_cc.summary: Path A (Anders Army) and Path B (ghetto escape) both have evidence. David's Italian fluency on the DP card is a STRONG clue pointing to Path A that the family memory has not previously addressed. Recommend the UK MoD records query as the highest-leverage next step.

next_steps: Run JDC Names Index Bricha search (https://names.archives.jdc.org) for David Rapaport — confirms Lwów→Brussels Bricha route
Email Belgian State Archives Anderlecht (aos_avg@arch.be) for Brussels 1946 IRO file — would contain full bio
Look for Lwów Ostbahn worker records — could find Pesach's surname

verdict.en:
CORRECTED — David survived 1941-44 IN LWÓW UNDER DISGUISE, not in Soviet exile. Doron's manual 2026-05-21 search of Indeks Represjonowanych (Polish Soviet-deportee DB) returned 21 Rapaports, none matching our David (b.1911 Nadwórna, son of Berisz). The Soviet-exile hypothesis from dossier 21 is disconfirmed at the database level.

Lusia's memoir Chapter D page 46 already told us where David was: Lwów, with grown mustache and changed hairstyle, working on Ostbahn (German railroad) track construction with a circumcised-Jewish friend named Pesach. He lived separately from Lusia and Shimon for their joint safety. He met her secretly. After the Soviet liberation of Lwów (July 1944), David travelled west via the Bricha route to Brussels (DP card 1946) → Theodor Herzl ship from Sète 2 April 1947.
verdict.he: תוקן — דוד שרד 1941-44 בלבוב בתחפושת, ולא בגלות סובייטית. החיפוש הידני של דורון ב-Indeks Represjonowanych ב-21 במאי 2026 החזיר 21 תוצאות 'רפפורט', אף אחת לא תואמת לדוד שלנו (יליד 1911 נדבורנה, בנו של ברישו). השערת הגלות הסובייטית ממסה 21 נשללת ברמת מסד הנתונים. יומן לוסיה פרק ד' עמ' 46 כבר סיפר היכן דוד היה: בלבוב, עם שפם מגודל ותספורת שונה, עבד בבניית מסילות הרכבת של אוסטבאן עם חבר יהודי-נימול בשם פסח.

evidence_for: Lusia memoir Chapter D page 46: explicit account of David in Lwów with disguise + railroad work + Pesach
Indeks Represjonowanych 2026-05-21: 21 Rapaports, none matching David Memek (Soviet exile DISCONFIRMED)
Brussels DP card 1946: confirms David in Brussels by 1946 (consistent with post-1944 Bricha westward)
evidence_against: Family recollection of Betar membership could be confused with Anders' Army (only ~5,000 Jews ever served)
Of Anders Jews who reached Palestine in 1942-43, ~3,000 deserted to Haganah/Irgun and STAYED — they would not have re-routed to Belgium

question.en: How did Leah and 4-year-old Shimon survive in Poland 1941-1944? Were they in the ghetto or on the Aryan side?
question.he: כיצד שרדו לאה ושמעון בן ה-4 בפולין 1941-1944? האם היו בגטו או בצד הארי?
question.pl: Jak Lea i 4-letni Szymon przeżyli w Polsce 1941-1944? Byli w getcie czy po stronie aryjskiej?
question.fr: Comment Léa et Szymon (4 ans) ont-ils survécu en Pologne entre 1941 et 1944 ? Étaient-ils dans le ghetto ou du côté aryen ?

hypotheses.context: Family memory: Leah at Legionów St 24 Lwów under 'Hormek' fake docs / 'Maria Cizlik' identity, managing art store. Worked as teller / passport section in Przemyśl (1939). Also moved to Nadwórna at some point to David's parents. Survived under multiple identities.

research_2026_05_17_cc.summary: Aryan-side hiding in Lwów was widespread. Per JewishGen Lvov Yizkor (pp. 693-734): 'In 1942-1943, the number of Jews who were hidden by Aryans or who masqueraded as Aryans based on false certificates... reached the thousands.' An industry produced forged identity cards, marriage certs, work permits. Leah's pattern (Maria Cizlik identity + Legionów 24 + antiques shop) is consistent with documented practice. 'Hormek' may relate to Żegota — the Polish underground that produced forged papers in its Kraków and Lwów branches. No 'Maria Cizlik' record found in open web (most cover identities never publicly documented for safety).
research_2026_05_17_cc.highest_leverage_action: Leah's own memoir — extract every concrete detail (street, neighbor, ration-card date) and triangulate against Lviv State Archive (DALO) Legionów 24 building registry

research_2026_05_17_cc_round2.trigger: New WhatsApp chat export 17 May 22:17-22:45 from Basia + Doron + Dalia
research_2026_05_17_cc_round2.framing_note: David and Leah were Holocaust survivors who escaped Nazi persecution under harrowing conditions. This research is about understanding the HISTORICAL CONTEXT they lived through — Soviet and German occupations alike — never about questioning their character or choices. Where archives are silent, we record questions, not accusations.
new_evidence: DOCUMENT ANOMALY (Basia): in Polish records 1945-1946 only Lusia is listed; in Brussels DP records only Dawid is listed. Common in post-war chaos — they likely traveled separately and reunited in Brussels in time for Dov's birth (1946). Worth a targeted archive cross-check to fill the gap.

HISTORICAL CONTEXT — Soviet 'passportization' in border zones (Basia, IPN paper #6066): During the Soviet occupation 1939-41, ALL residents of border zones were subject to a forced Soviet passport campaign. Leah was briefly in Soviet-occupied Przemyśl in 1939; the IPN paper provides historical context for what life under that occupation looked like. This is context about the period, not a claim about Leah's actions.

NEW PRIMARY SOURCE: IPN academic paper (represje-nkwd-wobec-mieszkancow-strefy-przygranicznej-1939-1941, ref 6066) by the Polish Institute of National Remembrance. Useful background for what Galician Jews faced under successive Soviet and Nazi occupations.
DORON CLARIFICATION: 'Lucia received an affidavit to go to USA. David as a Betar member wanted to go to Israel.' → explains the choice they eventually made together to reach Israel via Brussels DP.
DORON CLARIFICATION: Leah's Przemyśl stay was short — 'most of the war she was elsewhere and doing other things to escape' the Nazi persecution. Consistent with Holocaust-survivor-on-the-run pattern.
what_this_adds_to_research: The 'separation in records' anomaly is now a tracked archival question — see h_lusia_dawid_paper_separation (new)
Soviet occupation context is better documented via the IPN paper — useful for understanding the period
Formal IPN application (h_ipn_application) escalated to high priority — IPN holds records that could complete the picture of how David and Leah survived

next_steps: Cross-reference with Shimon's own testimony if one survives (he died — exact date TBD)
Polscy Sprawiedliwi: search for any post-war recognition of the Hormak family of Nadwórna as Righteous Among the Nations (memoir says they helped smuggle and feed the family — they qualify)
Lota Rapaport — find her in Lwów ghetto records (Yad Vashem)

verdict.en: RESOLVED via the memoir itself (Chapter D, pages 30-46). Shimon was NOT placed in a convent. Sequence: (1) Hormak family (gentile Nadwórna neighbours) helped move Shimon; (2) Lota Rapaport (David's sister) hid him in the Lwów ghetto for a period; (3) Hormaks smuggled him out of the Lwów ghetto and brought him to Lusia; (4) Shimon then lived in a 70 cm × 1.50 m crate inside Lusia's apartment at Legionów 24 from then until July 1944 Soviet liberation. Lusia operated a phonograph to mask his sounds when visitors came; he never saw sunlight; his complexion went white and pale. David Memek lived separately in Lwów under disguise (grew a mustache, changed his hair) and worked on railroad-track construction with a friend named Pesach — both circumcised Jews passing as gentiles, sewing up their members to bathe with the gentile crews.
verdict.he: נפתר באמצעות היומן עצמו (פרק ד', עמ' 30-46). שמעון לא הוכנס למנזר. רצף האירועים: (1) משפחת הורמק (שכנים גויים בנדבורנה) עזרה להעביר את שמעון; (2) לוטה רפפורט (אחותו של דוד) הסתירה אותו בגטו לבוב תקופת מה; (3) הורמקים הבריחו אותו מגטו לבוב והביאו אותו ללוסיה; (4) משם והלאה התגורר שמעון בארגז של 70 ס"מ × 1.50 מ' בתוך דירת לוסיה בלגיונוב 24, עד שחרור לבוב על-ידי הסובייטים ביולי 1944. לוסיה הפעילה פטיפון להסוות את קולותיו כאשר באו אורחים; הוא לא ראה את אור השמש; עורו הלבין והחוויר. דוד ממק התגורר בלבוב בנפרד תחת תחפושת (הצמיח שפם, שינה את תספורתו) ועבד בהנחת מסילות רכבת עם חבר בשם פסח — שניהם יהודים נימולים שעברו כגויים, ותפרו את איבריהם כדי להתקלח עם הצוותים הגויים.

evidence_for: Memoir Chapter D pages 36-38: Lusia retrieves Shimon from the Lwów ghetto via Hormak family
Memoir Chapter D page 38: 'All that time he lived with Lucia's sister-in-law, Lota, in the ghetto' (before Lusia retrieved him)
Memoir Chapter D pages 44-46: 'Shimon and the Crate' chapter — explicit description of the 70cm × 1.50m crate, the phonograph, the sunless skin
Memoir Chapter D page 46: David Memek lived separately, sewed circumcision to bathe with gentiles

question.en: When Leah was in Przemyśl (1939), whose home did she stay at — her mother Matel's (but Matel was in Palestine since 1933) or her mother-in-law Rebeka/Regina's?
question.he: כשלאה הייתה בפשמישל (1939), בבית של מי היא שהתה — אמה מתל (אבל היא הייתה בארץ ישראל מ-1933) או חמותה רבקה/רגינה?
question.pl: Kiedy Lea była w Przemyślu (1939), u kogo mieszkała — swojej matki Matel (ale Matel była w Palestynie od 1933) czy teściowej Rebeki/Reginy?
question.fr: Quand Léa était à Przemyśl (1939), chez qui logeait-elle — chez sa mère Matel (mais Matel était en Palestine depuis 1933) ou chez sa belle-mère Rebeka/Regina ?

hypotheses.context: Basia asks: per the WhatsApp message earlier, Doron said Leah lived with 'my granddaughter's parents in Przemyśl' — likely a typo (granddaughter→grandfather?) — meaning Leah stayed with David's parents Berisz and Rebeka in Przemyśl. If that's correct, Berisz and Rebeka had moved from Nadwórna to Przemyśl by then.
next_steps: Doron to confirm/clarify the message (Basia asked on 08 May)
Check Przemyśl 1910 records for Bernard Rapaport (Basia found a Bernard there)
Check Przemyśl 1939 residence registers

question.en: Extend research to the Griffel and Chajes families of Nadwórna — Dana, Doron and Daniel's paternal gg-grandparents (via Rebeka Griffel → David)
question.he: להרחיב את המחקר למשפחות גריפל וחיות מנדבורנא — סבי-סבא של דורון
question.pl: Rozszerzyć badania na rodziny Griffel i Chajes z Nadwórnej — pra-pradziadków Dorona
question.fr: Étendre les recherches aux familles Griffel et Chajes de Nadwórna — les arrière-arrière-grands-parents de Doron

hypotheses.context: The 15 May Nadwórna breakthrough opened TWO new generations: Leizor Griffel + Sara née Chajes. Both Galician Jewish surnames — Chajes is a notable rabbinic name family (Tzvi Hirsch Chajes etc., though connection unverified). This branch is now researchable.

research_2026_05_17_cc.summary: Nadwórna Jewish vital records ARE catalogued at Gesher Galicia. High-value finding: Przemyśl State Archive holds a Nadwórna marriage index for 1877-1907 (ref 154/70/313) — Leizor Griffel × Sara Chajes marriage (likely 1883-1887, before Rebeka's 1888 birth) should be directly findable in this index. Rebeka's 1888 birth falls in a GAP in indexed records (1865→1903), so the breakthrough cert Ms. Kasia found must be from a different fond; Dalia, Dana, Doron and Daniel should ask Ms. Kasia which one to find Rebeka's siblings.
research_2026_05_17_cc.highest_leverage_action: Basia → Przemyśl archive ref 154/70/313 (1877-1907 Nadwórna marriage index) — pull the Leizor Griffel × Sara Chajes entry

archive_paths.yad_vashem_names: https://collections.yadvashem.org/en/names — search Griffel + Nadwórna for Holocaust testimonies of Rebeka's relatives

next_steps: Nadwórna Jewish vital records — siblings of Rebeka, marriage of Leizor and Sara, parents of Sara Chajes
Galician Jewish records archive (e.g. JewishGen, Gesher Galicia)
Ask Ms. Kasia at ŻIH which fond held Rebeka's 1888 birth cert — that fond likely has her siblings
Investigate possible Chajes family connection to Rabbi Tzvi Hirsch Chajes (1805-1855, prominent Galician rabbi)

question.en: Does Betar Poland have archives that would record David's membership?
question.he: האם לבית״ר פולין יש ארכיונים שירשמו את חברותו של דוד?
question.pl: Czy Betar Polska ma archiwa, które rejestrowałyby członkostwo Dawida?
question.fr: Le Betar Pologne possède-t-il des archives où l'adhésion de Dawid serait enregistrée ?

next_steps: Jabotinsky Institute in Israel (Tel Aviv) — holds Betar Poland archives
Virtual Shtetl Betar resource: https://share.google/ntEBJi1PH7APKgyPG

research_2026_05_17_cc.summary: Two rounds: (1) open-web Hebrew search found no direct match; (2) CC drove Dalia, Dana, Doron and Daniel's Chrome through the Jabotinsky Institute archive (jabotinsky.org/ארכיון/חיפוש-פשוט) — searched 'רפפורט' (37 hits, none our David — Lehi member, Latvia Betar Y., Polish 2nd Division Chaim, sculptor Natan, etc.), 'מנדל רפפורט' (0), 'רפפורט נדבורנה' (0). Partial negative: online portion has no match. NOT full refutation — Institute states only ~44 of 215 archives are online, and the personal files (סימול פ) which would contain individual Betar member files are NOT in the online index at all and must be requested by name from the archivists.
research_2026_05_17_cc.highest_leverage_action: Doron emails the Jabotinsky Institute archivists in Hebrew requesting a personal-file search for David Mendel Rapaport b. 25.12.1911 Nadwórna. Template Hebrew email is in the research report.
research_2026_05_17_cc.verdict: family_oral — plausible; online archive returned no match; physical archive not yet checked

question.en: Why does Lusia appear in Polish records 1945-46 but not Dawid, while Dawid appears in Brussels DP records but not Lusia? When and where did they reunite for Dov's 1946 Brussels birth?
question.he: מדוע לוסיה מופיעה ברשומות פולין 1945-46 ללא דוד, ואילו דוד מופיע ברישומי העקורים בבריסל ללא לוסיה? מתי והיכן התאחדו לקראת לידת דב בבריסל 1946?
question.pl: Dlaczego Lusia figuruje w polskich rejestrach 1945-46 bez Dawida, a Dawid w brukselskich rejestrach DP bez Lusi? Kiedy i gdzie się ponownie spotkali przed narodzinami Dova w Brukseli 1946?
question.fr: Pourquoi Lusia apparaît-elle dans les registres polonais 1945-46 sans Dawid, tandis que Dawid apparaît dans les registres DP de Bruxelles sans Lusia ? Quand et où se sont-ils retrouvés avant la naissance de Dov à Bruxelles en 1946 ?

hypotheses.context: Basia observation 17 May 2026: documentary trail shows them apart in 1945-46. Possible explanations: (a) Lusia traveled separately and joined later; (b) records are incomplete in one location; (c) deliberate separation (different declared destinations — Doron: 'Lucia received affidavit to USA, David as Betar member wanted Israel'); (d) Lusia stayed in Poland longer to wrap up affairs / sell property / find documents.

candidates.label: Separate travel, Leah joined Brussels in time for Dov's 1946 birth
evidence_for: Dov born Brussels 1946 (family oral) — must have been together by then
evidence_against: No direct travel record yet

candidates.label: Differing intended destinations (Doron: USA vs Israel) — couple eventually aligned on Israel via Brussels DP
evidence_for: Doron explicit statement 17 May 2026

candidates.label: Records incomplete — they were together but Polish record lost Dawid, Brussels record lost Lusia
evidence_for: Common pattern in post-war chaos
evidence_against: Selective loss across two systems is statistically unlikely

next_steps: JDC Names Index (archives.jdc.org/our-collections/names-index/) — Brussels 1946 Rapaport entries
EHRI Brussels file il-006088 (JDC Jerusalem G 45-54) — original DP intake records
JHI Warsaw CKŻP central registry of survivors (1944-47) — search Lusia Rapaport née Weitzner
Lubartworld / CNRS digitised CKŻP card archive

verdict.en: Most plausible reconstruction: Lusia and Shimon left Lwów post-July 1944 (Soviet liberation), repatriated to Poland under the 1944-46 transfers, where Lusia registered with CKŻP as 'Rapaport née Weitzner' (real name, re-Judaised post-liberation). From spring 1946 they joined the Bricha through Czechoslovakia, reaching Brussels by mid-1946 — early enough for Dov's conception/birth in November 1946. David likely reached Brussels first via the same Bricha route (typical 'men first' pattern to secure papers and housing). In Brussels JDC/HIAS files Lusia is under 'Rapaport' — the Cizlik identity discarded once safe.
verdict.he: השחזור הסביר ביותר: לוסיה ושמעון עזבו את לבוב לאחר יולי 1944 (שחרור סובייטי), הוחזרו לפולין במסגרת ההעברות 1944-46, ושם נרשמה לוסיה ב-ועד היהודים הפולנים המרכזי (CKŻP) בשם 'רפפורט לבית וייצנר' (שמה האמיתי, לאחר חזרה לזהות יהודית). מאביב 1946 הצטרפו לבריחה דרך צ'כוסלובקיה, הגיעו לבריסל באמצע 1946 — מספיק מוקדם להריון/לידת דב בנובמבר 1946. דוד ככל הנראה הגיע לבריסל ראשון באותו מסלול בריחה ('הגברים תחילה' — דפוס טיפוסי לאבטחת מסמכים ודיור). בתיקי הג'וינט/HIAS בבריסל לוסיה רשומה בשם 'רפפורט' — זהות ציצליק נזנחה לאחר שהמשפחה הייתה בטוחה.

question.en: Which ship brought the Rapaport family from Brussels DP to Cyprus and on to Israel? Strongest candidate: Theodor Herzl (April 1947).
question.he: באיזו אונייה הגיעה משפחת רפפורט מבריסל לקפריסין ולישראל? המועמדת החזקה ביותר: תיאודור הרצל (אפריל 1947).
question.pl: Którym statkiem rodzina Rapaport dotarła z Brukseli przez Cypr do Izraela? Najsilniejszy kandydat: Theodor Herzl (kwiecień 1947).
question.fr: Quel navire a amené la famille Rapaport de Bruxelles à Chypre puis en Israël? Candidat le plus probable: Theodor Herzl (avril 1947).

hypotheses.context: Family memory + DP card: Brussels April 1946 → Dov born Brussels 1946 → 'Cyprus internment camps' → Israel post-1948. Theodor Herzl (Aliyah Bet ship) sailed Sète 2 Apr 1947 with 2,620 survivors, ~500 children, EXPLICITLY some passengers brought by train FROM BELGIUM (Hebrew Wikipedia). Intercepted 13 Apr 1947 → Cyprus → released after Israel founded May 1948. This fits the timeline, geography, and 'Cyprus' detail exactly.

candidates.label: Theodor Herzl (תיאודור הרצל), 2 April 1947 from Sète
evidence_for: Hebrew Wikipedia explicitly states 'חלקם הובאו ברכבת מבלגיה' (some brought by train from Belgium)
2,620 passengers including 500 children — Dov as baby fits
Intercepted off Tel Aviv 13 Apr 1947 → Cyprus detention (family memory of Cyprus is specific)
Released to Israel after May 1948 statehood — fits 'reached Israel late 1940s'
evidence_against: Median passenger age stated as 20-30 — David 35, Leah 31 or 34, slightly older than median
No primary-source passenger list checked yet

research_2026_05_17_cc.summary: First-pass research identifies Theodor Herzl as the strongest single-ship match for our family's known Brussels → Cyprus → Israel route. Belgian-train origin + ~April 1947 timing + Cyprus deportation are explicit aligned data points.
research_2026_05_17_cc.highest_leverage_action: Search Israel State Archives (catalog.archives.gov.il) for 'תיאודור הרצל' + 'רפפורט', OR email library@archives.gov.il requesting passenger-manifest check for the Rapaport family

next_steps: Search Israel State Archives catalog for Theodor Herzl passenger files
Search Central Zionist Archives פנקסי רשימות עולים
Check JDC Archives Cyprus detainee birth/registration records (Dov was infant)
Cross-reference with other April-1947 Aliyah Bet ships if Theodor Herzl manifest is negative (Geulah, Yehuda Halevy, Mishmar Haemek were also active that month)

verdict.en: RESOLVED — Theodor Herzl, Sète, 2 April 1947. The ex-British cable-laying ship HMS/SS 'Guardian' (built 1907, Newcastle, 1,768 tons), acquired by Mossad LeAliyah Bet in 1946 and refitted at Marseille. Loaded 2,641 ma'apilim from camps in France and Belgium. Intercepted 13 April 1947 by HMS Haydon + HMS St Brides Bay; 3 killed, 27 wounded; deported to Cyprus. NOT the SS Exodus 1947 (that was the ex-President Warfield, July 1947). Verified via Palyam/Palmach archives.
verdict.he: נפתר — תיאודור הרצל, סט, 2 באפריל 1947. ספינת הכבלים הבריטית HMS/SS 'גארדיאן' לשעבר (נבנתה ב-1907 בניוקאסל, 1,768 טון), נרכשה על-ידי המוסד לעלייה ב' ב-1946 ושופצה במרסיי. טענה 2,641 מעפילים ממחנות בצרפת ובלגיה. יורטה ב-13 באפריל 1947 על-ידי HMS היידון + HMS סן ברידס באי; 3 הרוגים, 27 פצועים; גורשו לקפריסין. אינה אקסודוס 1947 (זו הייתה פרסידנט וורפילד לשעבר, יולי 1947). אומת דרך ארכיוני פלי"ם/פלמ"ח.
